message:
feat(runtime,render,widgets): add snapshot undo store, diff strategy enrichment, and evidence ledger tests

ftui-runtime:
- Add snapshot_store.rs implementing a persistent undo snapshot store
  with configurable retention, size-bounded storage, and crash-safe
  serialization for the undo/redo system
- Expand conformal_predictor with richer calibration tracking and
  evidence emission for frame-time prediction quality monitoring
- Expand eprocess_throttle with adaptive throttle parameters and
  structured telemetry for e-process rate limiting
- Wire snapshot store and new modules into program.rs and undo/mod.rs
- Add proptest and tempfile dev-dependencies for property testing

ftui-render:
- Enrich diff_strategy.rs with additional diff heuristics for
  detecting cell-level changes more precisely, reducing unnecessary
  terminal writes in the incremental rendering path
- Update frame.rs with minor rendering pipeline adjustments

ftui-widgets:
- Expand command_palette/scorer.rs with richer fuzzy matching
  heuristics and score normalization
- Add structured evidence annotations to help.rs
- Expand hint_ranker.rs with additional ranking signals and
  tie-breaking logic for hint display prioritization

Tests:
- e2e_evidence_ledger.rs: end-to-end tests validating evidence
  ledger emission across runtime lifecycle events
- e2e_snapshot_store.rs: end-to-end tests for snapshot store
  persistence, recovery, and size-bounding behavior
- proptest_snapshot_store_invariants.rs: property-based tests
  verifying snapshot store invariants under random operations

Beads issue tracker synced.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"bd-2alzw.5","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for Per-Frame Arena Allocation","description":"## E2E Integration Test: Per-Frame Arena Allocation (EV=24)

### Scope
Full integration test rendering all demo screens with bumpalo-based per-frame arena allocation on the render hot path. Validates that arena allocation is transparent (identical output) and logs allocation metrics per frame.

### Test Scenario
1. Initialize FrankenTUI with per-frame BumpArena enabled (bumpalo::Bump thread-local).
2. Render all demo screens for 10 frames each.
3. On each frame boundary, log arena usage before reset.
4. Compare every rendered frame hash against baseline (standard allocator).
5. Stress test: render complex nested widget trees (50+ depth) to exercise arena capacity.
6. Verify arena reset occurs exactly once per frame boundary.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each frame emits one JSONL line:
```json
{
  "event": "arena_frame",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "frame_id": u64,
  "screen": "string",
  "arena_alloc_count": u32,
  "arena_bytes_used": u64,
  "arena_bytes_capacity": u64,
  "arena_reset": bool,
  "frame_hash": "hex_string",
  "baseline_match": bool,
  "alloc_time_ns": u64
}
```

### Success Criteria
- Zero visual diff: every frame_hash matches baseline (standard allocator).
- arena_reset == true exactly once per frame.
- No heap allocations on the hot render path (alloc_count from standard allocator == 0 during arena scope).
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)], bumpalo is safe API only).
- JSONL log is schema-compliant and parseable.
- Memory usage does not grow across frames (arena resets properly).","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:06.614363380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:26:56.538424062Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2alzw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2alzw","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:06.614363380Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-2alzw.5","depends_on_id":"bd-2alzw.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:26:56.538347760Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

 {"id":"bd-3hsz","title":"Text Effects: Shadow and Multi-Layer Rendering","description":"## Background
Flat text lacks depth. Shadows and multi-layer rendering add dimension, improve readability on complex backgrounds, and create professional polish.

## Implementation

Add Shadow struct:
```rust
pub struct Shadow {
    pub dx: i8,
    pub dy: i8,
    pub color: PackedRgba,
    pub opacity: f64,
}
```

Add GlowConfig for multi-layer glow:
```rust
pub struct GlowConfig {
    pub color: PackedRgba,
    pub intensity: f64,    // 0.0-1.0
    pub layers: u8,        // Number of glow layers (1-5)
    pub falloff: f64,      // How quickly glow fades (0.5 = 50% per layer)
}
```

Add to StyledText:
- .shadow(Shadow) - single drop shadow
- .shadows(Vec<Shadow>) - multiple shadow layers
- .glow(GlowConfig) - multi-layer soft glow

NOTE: Outline/stroke is in separate bd-882b bead to keep concerns separate.

Rendering approach:
1. Render shadow/glow layers first (furthest from text first)
2. Each layer offset by (dx*layer, dy*layer) with decreasing opacity
3. Render main text on top
4. Handle edge cases: shadows near terminal bounds use saturating arithmetic

## Files
crates/ftui-extras/src/text_effects.rs

## Unit Tests (REQUIRED)
- test_shadow_offset_applied: Shadow appears at dx,dy from text
- test_shadow_opacity: Shadow color correctly alpha-blended
- test_shadow_multiple_layers: Vec<Shadow> renders all layers
- test_glow_layer_count: Correct number of glow layers rendered
- test_glow_falloff: Each layer dimmer by falloff factor
- test_shadow_at_edge: No panic at terminal boundaries
- test_shadow_z_order: Main text renders on top of all shadows
- test_glow_intensity_scales: Higher intensity = brighter glow

## Visual Tests
- Render 'SHADOW' with drop shadow (1,1), verify offset
- Render 'GLOW' with 3-layer glow, verify falloff pattern
- Render at terminal edge, verify no overflow

## Acceptance Criteria
- Deliver the behavior implied by the title: Text Effects: Shadow and Multi-Layer Rendering
- Alien Artifact: define invariants + failure modes; if heuristics exist, specify an explainable decision rule (evidence ledger / Bayes factors where applicable)
- Extreme Optimization: baseline -> profile -> opportunity matrix; for each perf change include an isomorphism proof + golden output checksums
- Unit tests: happy path + edge cases + regression fixtures; add property tests for core invariants where applicable
- E2E: scripts with verbose JSONL logging (env, capabilities, timings, seed, checksums) + deterministic mode

-{"id":"bd-3hvkz.4","title":"task(e2e): E2E test suite for HAMT Undo/Redo","description":"## E2E Integration Test: HAMT Undo/Redo (EV=10.7)

### Scope
Full integration test performing edit/undo/redo sequences on editor widget using HAMT-backed persistent state. Validates exact state restoration and logs snapshot memory overhead.

### Test Scenario
1. Initialize editor widget with HAMT-backed SnapshotStore.
2. Perform 100 sequential edits (insert char, delete line, paste block, format, etc.).
3. Undo all 100 edits one at a time, verifying state matches expected at each step.
4. Redo all 100 edits, verifying state matches original edit sequence.
5. Perform random undo/redo interleaving (50 random jumps).
6. Test on form widget and tree widget state (not just editor).
7. Verify structural sharing: memory does not grow linearly with snapshot count.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each undo/redo operation emits:
```json
{
  "event": "hamt_undo_redo",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "operation": "edit|undo|redo",
  "step": u32,
  "snapshot_count": u32,
  "memory_bytes": u64,
  "shared_nodes": u32,
  "total_nodes": u32,
  "sharing_ratio": f64,
  "state_hash": "hex_string",
  "expected_hash": "hex_string",
  "match": bool,
  "op_time_ns": u64
}
```

### Success Criteria
- match == true for every undo/redo step (exact state restoration).
- sharing_ratio >= 0.90 after 10+ edits (>= 90% structural sharing).
- Memory growth is O(log n) per snapshot, not O(n).
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
- JSONL log is schema-compliant and parseable.
- Undo/redo operations complete in < 1ms each.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:49.509193423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:02.491082130Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","logging","test"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hvkz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3hvkz","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:24:49.509193423Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-3hvkz.4","depends_on_id":"bd-3hvkz.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-13T22:27:02.491011287Z","created_by":"ubuntu","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}

### Scope
Full integration test performing edit/undo/redo sequences on editor widget using HAMT-backed persistent state. Validates exact state restoration and logs snapshot memory overhead.

### Test Scenario
1. Initialize editor widget with HAMT-backed SnapshotStore.
2. Perform 100 sequential edits (insert char, delete line, paste block, format, etc.).
3. Undo all 100 edits one at a time, verifying state matches expected at each step.
4. Redo all 100 edits, verifying state matches original edit sequence.
5. Perform random undo/redo interleaving (50 random jumps).
6. Test on form widget and tree widget state (not just editor).
7. Verify structural sharing: memory does not grow linearly with snapshot count.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each undo/redo operation emits:

```json
  "total_nodes": u32,
  "sharing_ratio": f64,
  "state_hash": "hex_string",
  "expected_hash": "hex_string",
  "match": bool,
  "op_time_ns": u64
}
```

- match == true for every undo/redo step (exact state restoration).

 {"id":"bd-3hy","title":"Define per-module unit test coverage requirements","description":"# Testing: Unit Test Coverage Matrix (per crate/module)

## Purpose
This bead defines **explicit, per-crate unit test expectations** so we can enforce correctness while the codebase grows.

This is intentionally opinionated: the kernel is the product, so kernel crates have higher coverage requirements.

## How To Use
- When adding a new module, add it to the matrix.
- When adding a new public API, add explicit unit tests here.
- CI should enforce these thresholds (see bd-xn2).

---

## Coverage Targets (v1)
These are *minimum* targets. For kernel-critical modules, we should aim higher.

- ftui-render: ≥ 85% (kernel)
- ftui-core: ≥ 80% (terminal/session + input)
- ftui-style: ≥ 80%
- ftui-text: ≥ 80%
- ftui-layout: ≥ 75%
- ftui-runtime: ≥ 75%
- ftui-widgets: ≥ 70%
- ftui-extras: ≥ 60% (feature-gated; correctness still required, but scope is optional)

Note: Some integration-heavy paths (PTY tests) will not show up as unit coverage; they are enforced separately.

---

## ftui-render (≥ 85%)
Kernel correctness lives here.

### Cell / CellContent / CellAttrs
- [ ] CellContent creation from char vs grapheme-id
- [ ] Width semantics (ASCII, wide, combining, emoji)
- [ ] Continuation-cell sentinel semantics for wide glyphs
- [ ] PackedRgba: construction + Porter-Duff alpha blending
- [ ] CellAttrs: bitflags operations + merge/override
- [ ] 16-byte Cell layout invariants (size/alignment) + bits_eq correctness

### Buffer
- [ ] Create/resize buffer with dimensions
- [ ] get/set bounds checking + deterministic defaults
- [ ] Clear semantics (full vs region)
- [ ] Scissor stack push/pop semantics (intersection monotonicity)
- [ ] Opacity stack push/pop semantics (product in [0,1])
- [ ] Wide glyph placement + continuation cells
- [ ] Iteration order and row-major storage assumptions

### Diff
- [ ] empty diff (no changes)
- [ ] single cell change
- [ ] row changes
- [ ] run grouping behavior
- [ ] scratch buffer reuse (no unbounded allocations)

### Presenter
- [ ] cursor tracking correctness
- [ ] style tracking correctness
- [ ] link tracking correctness (OSC 8 open/close)
- [ ] single-write-per-frame behavior
- [ ] synchronized output behavior where supported (fallback correctness)

---

## ftui-core (≥ 80%)

### Event types
- [ ] canonical key/mouse/resize/paste/focus event types are stable

### InputParser
- [ ] bounded CSI/OSC/DCS parsing (DoS limits)
- [ ] bracketed paste decoding + max size
- [ ] mouse SGR decoding
- [ ] focus/resize event decoding

### TerminalCapabilities
- [ ] env heuristic detection (TERM/COLORTERM)
- [ ] mux flags (tmux/screen/zellij) correctness

### TerminalSession lifecycle
- [ ] RAII enter/exit discipline
- [ ] panic cleanup paths are idempotent

---

## ftui-style (≥ 80%)
- [ ] Style defaults + builder ergonomics
- [ ] deterministic style merge (explicit masks)
- [ ] Color downgrade (truecolor→256→16→mono)
- [ ] Theme presets + semantic slots
- [ ] StyleSheet registry + named style composition

---

## ftui-text (≥ 80%)
- [ ] Segment system correctness (Cow<str>)
- [ ] width measurement correctness + LRU cache behavior
- [ ] grapheme segmentation helpers for wrap/truncate correctness
- [ ] wrap/truncate semantics for ZWJ/emoji/combining
- [ ] markup parser correctness (feature-gated)

---

## ftui-layout (≥ 75%)
- [ ] Rect operations (intersection/contains)
- [ ] Flex constraint solving + gaps
- [ ] Grid placement + spanning + named areas
- [ ] Measurement algebra (min/max + clamp)

---

## ftui-runtime (≥ 75%)
- [ ] Program/Model/Cmd lifecycle + scheduler
- [ ] determinism invariants (simulator)
- [ ] subscriptions/ticks integration

---

## ftui-widgets (≥ 70%)
Harness essentials must be tested.

- [ ] Panel/Block borders + title placement
- [ ] Paragraph wrapping + alignment
- [ ] Table sizing + selection
- [ ] Viewport/log viewer scrolling
- [ ] TextInput cursor + edit behavior
- [ ] Progress/spinner rendering

---

## Test Quality Requirements
- Each test is deterministic (no wall-clock timing).
- Each test is fast (target <10ms).
- Failures must be actionable (expected vs actual grid/segments, not “assertion failed”).

## Property Testing
Use property tests for:
- diff correctness
- presenter roundtrip via terminal model
- text width/wrap invariants
- layout solver invariants

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] The matrix matches the actual crate boundaries (no stale mapping).
- [ ] CI encodes these thresholds (or a deliberate waiver is documented).
- [ ] Adding a new module requires adding it to this matrix.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:29:14.532567931Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:36:01.816690672Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:36:01.816665214Z","close_reason":"Expanded coverage matrix with per-module expectations","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3hy","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":428,"issue_id":"bd-3hy","author":"ubuntu","text":"Reopened: Reopening per user request; keep issues open until explicitly agreed to close.","created_at":"2026-02-01T03:18:15Z"}]}

## Background
Bracketed paste mode prevents pasted content from being interpreted as commands. SGR mouse protocol provides precise mouse reporting with modifiers.

## Technical Requirements

### Bracketed Paste Mode
Escape sequences:
- Enable: ESC[?2004h
- Disable: ESC[?2004l
- Paste start: ESC[200~
- Paste end: ESC[201~

pub struct BracketedPasteParser {

}

impl BracketedPasteParser {

            // Enforce max_paste_size limit
        } else {
            // Check for paste start sequence
        }
    }
}

pub enum InputEvent {
    Key(KeyEvent),
    Mouse(MouseEvent),
    Paste(String),  // Complete paste content
    Resize(u16, u16),
    Focus(bool),
}

### SGR Mouse Protocol (1006)
Escape sequence: ESC[<Cb;Cx;CyM or ESC[<Cb;Cx;Cym

struct SgrMouseParser;

impl SgrMouseParser {
    /// Parse SGR extended mouse report
    pub fn parse(&self, params: &[u8]) -> Option<MouseEvent> {
        // Parse: <button;column;rowM or <button;column;rowm
        // Button encoding:
        //   0 = left, 1 = middle, 2 = right
        //   +4 = shift, +8 = meta, +16 = ctrl
        //   32 = motion, 64 = scroll
        // M = press, m = release
    }
}

pub struct MouseEvent {
    pub kind: MouseEventKind,
    pub column: u16,
    pub row: u16,
    pub modifiers: KeyModifiers,
}

pub enum MouseEventKind {
    Down(MouseButton),
    Up(MouseButton),
    Drag(MouseButton),
    Moved,
    ScrollDown,
    ScrollUp,
    ScrollLeft,
    ScrollRight,
}

### Enable/Disable Mouse
pub fn enable_mouse_capture(writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
    // Enable SGR extended mode (1006)
    // Enable any-event tracking (1003) or button-event (1002)
    write!(writer, "\x1b[?1000h\x1b[?1006h")?;
    Ok(())
}

pub fn disable_mouse_capture(writer: &mut impl Write) -> io::Result<()> {
    write!(writer, "\x1b[?1006l\x1b[?1000l")?;
    Ok(())
}

### Paste Size Limits
- Default max: 1MB
- Configurable via builder
- Large pastes: return error or truncate

### Focus Events (bonus)
- Enable: ESC[?1004h
- Disable: ESC[?1004l
- Focus in: ESC[I
- Focus out: ESC[O

- [ ] SGR mouse position decoding
- [ ] Modifier key detection
- [ ] Scroll events
- [ ] Motion events
- [ ] Focus events

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Bracketed paste with size limit
- [ ] SGR mouse protocol parsing
- [ ] All mouse event types
- [ ] Modifier key capture
- [ ] Focus in/out events
- [ ] Enable/disable APIs

 {"id":"bd-6e9.8","title":"Implement grapheme segmentation helpers for ftui-text","description":"# Implement grapheme segmentation helpers for ftui-text

## Background
Plan requirements:
- width correctness for emoji/ZWJ/combining marks is a stop-ship gate
- ftui-text needs grapheme segmentation helpers for wrapping/truncation/alignment

We should isolate Unicode segmentation logic behind a small set of helpers so:
- widgets/text algorithms stay readable
- we can swap implementation details without touching call sites

## Scope
Provide utilities built on `unicode-segmentation` + `unicode-width`:
- grapheme iteration
- grapheme-aware truncation to display width
- (optional) word boundaries / soft wrap points

Example APIs (shape, not necessarily final):
```rust
pub fn grapheme_count(s: &str) -> usize;
pub fn graphemes(s: &str) -> impl Iterator<Item = &str>;
pub fn display_width(s: &str) -> usize; // uses ASCII fast path where applicable (bd-v6y)

/// Returns (prefix, width) where prefix fits within max_width.
pub fn truncate_to_width(s: &str, max_width: usize) -> (&str, usize);
```

## Non-goals
- Full Unicode normalization/bidi/search subsystems (out of scope for plan v1)

## Tests
- Grapheme iteration matches expectations on:
  - ASCII
  - combining marks
  - emoji + ZWJ sequences
- truncate_to_width never splits mid-grapheme

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Helpers exist and are used by Text wrapping/truncation code.
- [ ] Unicode width corpus tests (bd-16k) can exercise these helpers.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria

### Functional Requirements
- [ ] grapheme_count: Count grapheme clusters in string
- [ ] grapheme_at: Get grapheme at index (0-based)
- [ ] grapheme_slice: Extract grapheme range
- [ ] word_boundaries: Iterator over word break positions
- [ ] is_word_char: Classify grapheme as word character
- [ ] display_width: Calculate display width in cells
- [ ] truncate_to_width: Truncate to max display width

### Unicode Requirements
- [ ] ZWJ sequences treated as single grapheme (👨‍👩‍👧 = 1)
- [ ] Combining marks attached to base (e + ́ = 1)
- [ ] Emoji with skin tone = 1 grapheme
- [ ] Regional indicators paired correctly (🇺🇸 = 1)
- [ ] Word boundaries per UAX #29
- [ ] Width per UAX #11 (East Asian Width)

### Performance Requirements
- [ ] grapheme_count: O(n) with < 10ns/grapheme
- [ ] display_width: O(n) with width cache hits
- [ ] truncate_to_width: O(n) worst case, O(log n) with cache

### Caching Requirements
- [ ] Width cache for common graphemes
- [ ] LRU eviction for cache management
- [ ] Cache hit rate > 90% for typical text

### Logging Requirements
- [ ] tracing::trace! for cache misses (debug builds)
- [ ] tracing::debug! for unusual grapheme clusters
- [ ] tracing::warn! for width lookup failures

### Test Coverage

- [ ] Corpus test: East Asian Width edge cases
- [ ] Property test: truncate never exceeds width

- [ ] Benchmark: ASCII fast path performance","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-31T22:58:45.504414906Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-01T18:47:37.182639121Z","closed_at":"2026-02-01T18:47:37.182616068Z","close_reason":"Completed: Added grapheme_count, graphemes, truncate_to_width_with_info, word_boundaries, word_segments helpers with tests and doc examples","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-10i.6","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-16k","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"bd-6e9.8","depends_on_id":"bd-v6y","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-03T14:32:18Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"bd-6e9.9","title":"Bubbles Widget Test Suite","description":"Comprehensive test suite for all bubbles widgets. This ensures every widget is thoroughly tested with unit tests, property tests, integration tests, and e2e scenarios.

## Testing Philosophy
Every widget must be tested at multiple levels:
1. **Unit tests** - Individual method correctness
2. **Property tests** - Invariant verification with random inputs
3. **Integration tests** - Widget composition and interaction
4. **E2E tests** - Full user workflow simulation
5. **Snapshot tests** - Visual regression detection

## Test Requirements by Widget

### TextArea (bd-6e9.1)
Unit tests:
- `test_insert_single_char` - Insert at various cursor positions
- `test_insert_multiline` - Insert text containing newlines
- `test_delete_backward` - Backspace at line start joins lines
- `test_delete_forward` - Delete at line end joins lines
- `test_cursor_movement_horizontal` - Left/right wraps at line boundaries
- `test_cursor_movement_vertical` - Up/down maintains visual column
- `test_selection_shift_arrow` - Selection with shift+arrows
- `test_selection_word` - Double-click word selection
- `test_selection_line` - Triple-click line selection
- `test_undo_single_char` - Undo groups by word boundaries
- `test_undo_paste` - Paste is single undo unit
- `test_redo_after_edit` - Redo branch is discarded on new edit
- `test_word_wrap_soft` - Soft wrap doesn't modify buffer
- `test_word_wrap_hard` - Hard wrap inserts actual newlines
- `test_viewport_scroll_cursor_follow` - Viewport follows cursor
- `test_unicode_grapheme_cluster` - ZWJ sequences treated as single char
- `test_char_limit_enforcement` - Can't exceed limit
- `test_validation_callback` - Validation prevents invalid input

Property tests:
- `prop_insert_delete_inverse` - Insert then delete restores state
- `prop_undo_redo_inverse` - Undo then redo restores state
- `prop_cursor_always_valid` - Cursor never outside buffer bounds
- `prop_selection_ordered` - Selection start <= end

 {"id":"bd-6qxh","title":"Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation","description":"# Frame Caching with Smart Invalidation

## Background & Motivation

Many widgets are static most of the time:
- Headers and footers
- Borders and decorations
- Rarely-changing status bars

Re-rendering them every frame wastes CPU.

**The solution:**
- Cache rendered widget output
- Invalidate on model change or resize
- Composite cached content with live content

**Relationship to existing beads:**
- Requires bd-10i.3.6 (Buffer with scissor/opacity stacks)
- Complements bd-1iqn (Render Budget)

## Technical Design

```rust
pub struct CachedWidget<W: Widget> {
    inner: W,
    cache: Option<CachedBuffer>,
    last_area: Option<Rect>,
    dirty: bool,
}

pub struct CachedBuffer {
    buffer: Buffer,
    hash: u64,  // Content hash for change detection
}

impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    pub fn new(widget: W) -> Self { ... }
    pub fn mark_dirty(&mut self) { self.dirty = true; }
    
    pub fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let needs_render = self.dirty 
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.is_none();
            
        if needs_render {
            let mut cache_buf = Buffer::empty(area);
            self.inner.render(area, &mut cache_buf);
            self.cache = Some(CachedBuffer::new(cache_buf));
            self.last_area = Some(area);
            self.dirty = false;
        }
        
        if let Some(cache) = &self.cache {
            buf.merge(&cache.buffer);
        }
    }
}
```

## Logging Requirements

```rust
// Cache hit (TRACE level)
tracing::trace!(widget = name, "Cache hit, using cached buffer");

// Cache miss (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, reason = ?reason, "Cache miss, re-rendering");

// Cache invalidated (DEBUG level)
tracing::debug!(widget = name, "Cache invalidated via mark_dirty()");
```

## E2E Tests

```bash
# Test cache hit rate
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-stats --duration 10s --static-widgets 5
# Assert: cache hit rate > 95%

# Test memory bounds
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-memory --cached-widgets 100
# Assert: memory < 10MB for 100 widgets

# Verify correctness
./ftui-harness --test-mode cache-verify --compare-to-uncached
# Assert: output identical
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] CachedWidget<W> wrapper implemented
- [ ] Caching works for static widgets
- [ ] Invalidation on mark_dirty()
- [ ] Resize triggers re-render
- [ ] Memory usage bounded
- [ ] Content hash detects changes
- [ ] Integration with widget tree
- [ ] No stale content ever displayed
- [ ] All tests pass","notes":"## Cache Safety Guidelines

### When to Use CachedWidget
- Static content (headers, footers, borders)
- Content that changes infrequently
- Expensive rendering operations

### When NOT to Use CachedWidget
- Dynamic content (logs, timers, animations)
- Content that depends on external state
- Small/fast widgets (caching overhead > render cost)

### Cache Coherence
The cache can become stale if:
1. Widget props change without mark_dirty()
2. External state affects rendering
3. Theme/style changes globally

Solution: Use automatic dirty detection via content hash:
```rust
impl<W: Widget + Hash> CachedWidget<W> {
    fn render(&mut self, area: Rect, buf: &mut Buffer) {
        let current_hash = self.inner.content_hash();
        
        let needs_render = self.dirty
            || self.last_area != Some(area)
            || self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.hash) != Some(current_hash);
        
        // ...
    }
}
```

### Memory Management
```rust
impl<W: Widget> CachedWidget<W> {
    /// Drop cache to free memory
    pub fn clear_cache(&mut self) {
        self.cache = None;
    }
    
    /// Check cache memory usage
    pub fn cache_size(&self) -> usize {
        self.cache.as_ref().map(|c| c.buffer.memory_size()).unwrap_or(0)
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-fbp","title":"Implement inline mode overlay redraw baseline","description":"PHASE 2.5 - INLINE MODE IMPLEMENTATION (CRITICAL)

## Background
Inline mode is a NON-NEGOTIABLE requirement. The overlay redraw baseline is the correctness foundation that works everywhere, before optimization.

## Technical Requirements (from Plan Section D)

### Overlay Redraw Strategy
1. Save cursor position (DECSC or manual tracking)
2. Move cursor to UI region start
3. Clear UI region only (never full screen!)
4. Render UI frame
5. Restore cursor position (DECRC)

### UI Region Anchoring
- Bottom-anchored (default): UI at bottom, logs scroll above
- Top-anchored (optional): UI at top, logs scroll below
- Configurable height: ui_height parameter

### Log Write Coordination
write_log() must:
1. Suspend UI temporarily
2. Print log line to scrollback region
3. Re-render UI in its anchored position
4. Maintain cursor contract

### Cursor Contract
After any present():
- Cursor is at the correct position per active policy
- UI region is intact
- Scrollback is preserved

### Never Clear Full Screen
The DECRC/ED2 pattern that clears full screen is FORBIDDEN in inline mode.
Only clear the bounded UI region using EL (erase line) or localized ED.

## Implementation

pub struct InlinePresenter {
    ui_height: u16,
    anchor: Anchor,  // Bottom or Top
    cursor_saved: bool,
}

impl InlinePresenter {
    pub fn present(&mut self, buffer: &Buffer, writer: &mut TerminalWriter) {
        // 1. Save cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b7")?;  // DECSC
        
        // 2. Move to UI region
        let (_, term_height) = writer.size();
        let ui_start = match self.anchor {
            Anchor::Bottom => term_height - self.ui_height,
            Anchor::Top => 0,
        };
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        
        // 3. Clear UI region only (line by line)
        for row in 0..self.ui_height {
            writer.write_all(b"\x1b[2K")?;  // EL - clear line
            if row < self.ui_height - 1 {
                writer.write_all(b"\n")?;
            }
        }
        
        // 4. Move back and render
        writer.move_to(0, ui_start)?;
        // ... emit diff ...
        
        // 5. Restore cursor
        writer.write_all(b"\x1b8")?;  // DECRC
    }
}

## Tests Required
- [ ] UI renders at correct position (bottom/top)
- [ ] Scrollback is preserved after present()
- [ ] Cursor returns to correct position
- [ ] Log writes don't corrupt UI
- [ ] Resize re-anchors correctly
- [ ] PTY test: verify no scrollback corruption

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Overlay redraw works on all terminals
- [ ] Cursor contract maintained
- [ ] UI region bounded, never full-screen clear
- [ ] Log coordination implemented
- [ ] PTY tests pass

## Performance Budget
- Overlay redraw: < 2ms (acceptable for correctness baseline)

6. For degradation: gradually increase frame times.
7. Collect all JSONL evidence entries and validate schema.

### JSONL Structured Logging Specification
Each decision point emits a unified evidence entry:
```json
{
  "event": "evidence_ledger",
  "ts_ns": u64,
  "decision_point": "diff_strategy|resize_coalesce|frame_budget|degradation|voi|hint_rank|cmd_score",
  "log_posterior": f64,
  "top_evidence": [
    { "term": "string", "bayes_factor": f64, "direction": "for|against" }
  ],
  "action_chosen": "string",
  "loss_avoided": f64,
  "confidence_interval": { "lower": f64, "upper": f64, "level": f64 },
  "alternatives_considered": u32,
  "frame_id": u64
}
```

### Success Criteria
- All 7 decision points emit at least one evidence entry during the test run.
- Every evidence entry has all required fields (no nulls in required fields).
- top_evidence array has exactly 3 entries per decision (top-3 evidence terms).
- bayes_factor values are positive and finite (no NaN/Inf).
- confidence_interval.lower < confidence_interval.upper.
- Schema validates against JSON Schema definition.
- No panics, no unsafe code (#![forbid(unsafe_code)]).
